title: 16S Illumina Primer Plate Resuspension (100 µM)
author: Rodolfo A Salido Benitez
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.ukgeutw
materials: ep.T.I.P.S. Motion Racks 20 - 300 µL w/ filter

steps.title: Add water to reservoir
steps.instruction: In a sterile 30mL reservoir, add 11.725 ml (required minimum volume) of PCR Clean Water to resuspend (4) 96-Well plates.

steps.title: Set up epMotion worktable
steps.instruction: Follow the diagram below while setting up the epMotion worktable. Place (4) boxes of ep.T.I.P.S. Motion Racks 20 - 300 µL w/ filter in slots A2-A5. Place (4) 96-Well plates of lyophilized primers from IDT in slots B2-B5. Place 30 mL reservoir with PCR Clean Water in slot 1 of the Resevoir Rack and place Rack in slot C5.

steps.title: Execute automated resuspension protocol
steps.instruction: Remove box lids and plate foils and execute protocol. (Protocol must be imported to epBlue software prior to attempting to execute it. epBlue 40.6 or later. Application_4_Resus_100uM_Primers_181003_105824.export6) The automated protocol transfers 30µL of PCR Clean Water into the (4) different 96-Well Primer Plates and mix pipettes for 5 cycles to resuspend lyophilized primers to a 100 µM.

steps.title: Centrifuge primer plates
steps.instruction: Centrifuge all 96-Well primer plates to ensure lyophilized sample is at the bottom of the well. (3nmol primer per well)

steps.title: Seal and store plates
steps.instruction: Remove plates from worktable and seal with storage aluminum foils.
